title: NOT working on Acer Nitro AN515-54
author: EmeraldStar0
body: Health mode appears to be unsupported, atleast on CachyOS. It can be enabled but doesn't function correctly, continuing to charge to 100%

title: Confirmed working: Acer Swift Go 14 (SFG14-71)
author: ash-negee
body:
## Laptop

- Acer Swift Go 14 (SFG14-71)

## Environment

- Ubuntu 26.04 LTS
- Linux 7.0.0-22-generic

## Results

The driver works correctly on this laptop.

- Module builds successfully.
- Module loads successfully.
- `health_mode` is available.
- Battery Health Mode can be enabled successfully.
- Battery charging stops at approximately 80% while connected to AC.

I also tested automatic enabling of Battery Health Mode after boot.

Thank you for maintaining this project!

title: Working on Acer Aspire 5-44G, running CachyOS
author: upgamerFranzo
body:
Hello, just reporting that the kernel module works on my Acer Aspire A515-44G.

I am using CachyOS. The module does not build correctly while using the default CachyOS kernel which is compiled with clang, but switching to a kernel compiled with GCC allows it to work as intended. Users can easily install a kernel compiled with GCC using Cachy's kernel manager, both cachyos-linux-gcc and the vanilla Arch linux kernel work. After changing the settings to limit charging you can go back to using a kernel compiled with clang and they remain active.

title: when will you upsteam to the kernel
author: olumolu
body: do you have any plan to upsteam it to linux kernel?

title: Aspire AL14-31P not working
author: aenagit
body:
After installing the module from the [Arch AUR](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/acer-wmi-battery-dkms), I rebooted my system and typed `echo 1 | sudo tee /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/health_mode`.
To test it, I typed `cat /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/health_mode`, which returned -1. I assumed this meant it wasn't working, but just to make sure, I discharged the battery to below 80% and plugged it again, but it simply recharged to 100% as usual.
The temperature function appears to be working, but `cat /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/temperature` always returns -270800.
I didn't try the calibration function.
I'd be glad to provide any other information.
***
From KDE Info Center:
Operating System: EndeavourOS 
KDE Plasma Version: 6.6.4
KDE Frameworks Version: 6.25.0
Qt Version: 6.11.0
Kernel Version: 7.0.3-arch1-1 (64-bit)
Graphics Platform: Wayland
Processors: 8 × Intel® Core™ i3-N300
Memory: 16 GiB of RAM (15,3 GiB usable)
Graphics Processor: Intel® Graphics
Manufacturer: Acer
Product Name: Aspire AL14-31P
System Version: V1.14

title: Works well on Acer Swift 1 (SF114-34-P3WR)
author: eh1511
body:
Works perfectly on an Acer Swift 1 (SF114-34-P3WR) with Arch / Manjaro / EndeavourOS.

The easiest way is to install it using any AUR helper (yay / paru, etc.):

`yay -S acer-wmi-battery-dkms`
(you may need to install linux headers before)

On KDE Plasma, the [Acer Battery Limit Switcher](https://store.kde.org/p/2284325) applet is ideal for toggling Health Mode on and off—no need to mess around in the terminal 😉

title: Working on Acer Aspire 5 A515-56
author: kamalkoranga
body:
Hi,

I wanted to report that the `acer-wmi-battery` driver works successfully on my system and share the exact steps I followed so others with similar setups can replicate it.

## System Details
- Laptop: Acer Aspire 5 (A515-56)
- OS: Fedora COSMIC Atomic 43
- Kernel: 6.19.13-200.fc43.x86_64

---

## Working Steps
**1: Install kernel-devel and kernel-headers**
```bash
sudo rpm-ostree install kernel-devel kernel-headers
systemctl reboot
```

---

**2: Clone the repo**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/frederik-h/acer-wmi-battery
cd acer-wmi-battery
```

---

**3: Build the module**
```bash
make
```

---

**4: Load the module**
```bash
sudo insmod acer-wmi-battery.ko
```

---

**5: Enable 80% charge limit**
```bash
echo 1 | sudo tee /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/health_mode
```
At this point, the battery stops charging at ~80% as expected.

> Note: This only works for the current session and resets after reboot.

---

## Permanent Fix (Fedora Atomic Workaround)
Since Fedora Atomic uses a read-only filesystem, the module cannot be installed in /lib/modules. Instead, I used systemd services.

---

**6: Create loader service**
```bash
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/acer-module.service
```

Paste this:
```
[Unit]
Description=Load Acer WMI Battery Module
After=multi-user.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/sbin/insmod ~/LOCATION/acer-wmi-battery/acer-wmi-battery.ko
RemainAfterExit=yes

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

> Note: Make sure `LOCATION` is set to the location you cloned the repo.
---

**7: Enable the loader service**
```bash
sudo systemctl enable acer-module.service
```

---

**8: Add health mode service**
```bash
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/acer-battery.service
```

Paste this:
```
[Unit]
Description=Enable Acer Battery Health Mode
After=acer-module.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/sh -c "echo 1 > /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/health_mode"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

---

**9: Enable the health mode service**
```bash
sudo systemctl enable acer-battery.service
```

---

**10**: Reboot and verify:
```bash
ls /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/
cat /sys/bus/wmi/drivers/acer-wmi-battery/health_mode
```

**Expected output:**
```bash
bind  calibration_mode  health_mode  module  temperature  uevent  unbind
1
```

At last Thank you @frederik-h for this great tool :).

title: Working on Acer Swift Go 14 SFG14-73T
author: fnimick
body:
Did not test calibration but confirmed charging stops at 80% with health mode enabled and continues to 100% with health mode disabled.

I also confirmed that setting health mode, putting the laptop to sleep at <80% charge, and plugging in for an extended period only charges the laptop to 80%.

Model number: SFG14-73T-75FA

OS: Bazzite x86_64 (based on fedora 43)
Host: Swift SFG14-73T (V1.19)
Kernel: Linux 6.17.7-ba29.fc43.x86_64
Display (CMN1459): 1920x1200 @ 1.25x in 14", 60 Hz [Built-in]
DE: GNOME 49.1
CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 7 155H (22) @ 4.80 GHz
GPU: Intel Arc Graphics @ 2.25 GHz [Integrated]

title: Working on Predator Helios Neo 16 (PHN16-72)
author: ViperSLM
body:
Thank you so much for writing this driver!
Confirmed that the acer-wmi-battery module works fine on this laptop. (Tested on Arch Linux, albeit on a custom kernel)

Health mode works fine and stops charging at 80% (although it would sometimes charge past it, i.e. stopping at 81%).
Haven't tested Calibration mode.

dmesg:
```
[    2.170503] acer_wmi_battery: loading out-of-tree module taints kernel.
[    2.170507] acer_wmi_battery: module verification failed: signature and/or required key missing - tainting kernel
[    2.196022] acer_wmi_battery: available modes: health mode, calibration mode
[    2.196027] acer_wmi_battery: active modes: health mode
[    3.525517] acer_wmi: Acer Laptop ACPI-WMI Extras
[    3.525550] acer_wmi: Function bitmap for Communication Button: 0x801
```

Specs:
```
OS: Arch Linux x86_64
Host: Predator PHN16-72 (V1.13)
Kernel: Linux 6.19.11-vslm-phn1672+
CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-14900HX (32) @ 5.80 GHz
GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Mobile [Discrete]
RAM: 32 GB DDR5 5600MHz
```
